description_long: This object is a vibrant cluster of orange-pink flowers with multiple blooms emerging from a central green stem. Each flower displays six elongated petals with a soft gradient from deep orange at the tips to a lighter, almost yellow hue near the base, and subtle streaks of white. The petals are slightly curved and overlap each other, creating a lush, layered appearance. The central part of each bloom is slightly recessed, with visible yellowish filaments and anthers. The stems are green and sturdy, branching out to support the dense floral cluster. No leaves are visible in the immediate vicinity of the flowers, emphasizing the prominence of the blossoms.
description: A dense cluster of orange-pink flowers with six elongated, overlapping petals per bloom, supported by green stems. The petals have a gradient from orange to yellow and display a lush, layered arrangement.
description_view_0: This view highlights the front-facing blooms, showing the open, overlapping petals and the central yellow filaments.
description_view_1: This angle reveals more of the cluster's depth, with several flowers overlapping and the stems partially visible beneath the petals.
description_view_2: The rear view emphasizes the backs of the petals, showing their curvature and the way they layer over each other, with less visibility of the flower centers.
description_view_3: This side view displays the profile of the blooms, with petals fanning outward and the green stems supporting the cluster.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration with each flower displaying six overlapping petals that radiate symmetrically from the center. The petals are slightly layered, creating a lush, circular bloom typical of many monocot flowers.
